body_txt: It's well-agreed that treachery is one of the worst things someone can experience . Rats, snitches, backstabbers, traitors, turncoats... so many words for the same reviled and feared archetype. Of course, everyone's the hero of their own story, so when one character decides to betray another character or a group, they may stop to give their two cents on the situation. Maybe they hated the target from the beginning , or now have a reason to and a way to act on it . Maybe they just got a better offer . Maybe, in a more heroic sense, they wanted to stop the target from going too far . One particularly nasty reason why the betrayer will switch sides is because they are a Dirty Coward who will do whatever it takes to save their own skin, even if it means betraying those whom they considered their friends. Needless to say, this is one of the least acceptable reasons for betrayal, as they will find themselves on the receiving end of a Cowardice Callout . At any rate, whether any other party wants to hear or accept the explanation or not, the betrayer will explain their reasons for committing the betrayal. This may lead to a Motive Rant . The traitor may try and explain themselves due to holding Abandonment-Induced Animosity towards their target. Compare with Daddy Had a Good Reason for Abandoning You . Contrast Post-Support Regret , where a character laments supporting someone who has betrayed their trust. Spoilers Off .
